title: Level 415
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 415: "Desolate White" }} {{User:The_Lone_Wanderer12313/CSSLEVEL415}}
{{Background|file=Level 415.22.png}}{{Alt Class|ent=3|env=5|ext=2|no=415}} Level 415 is the 416th level of the backrooms. = General Description = ----{{Blurimage|file=level 415.22.png|caption= The only picture of level 415.}}Level 415, known as ''Desolate White'', is a vast, frozen wasteland hidden within an endless forest. Temperatures remain locked below -35°C, and the snow is unusually dense and lethally cold so much so that visibility is often reduced to mere meters. Strangely, there’s no precipitation of any kind, leaving the snow’s origin unexplained. No plant life survives here, and no new growth has ever been recorded. Despite the terrain’s hostility, a web of artificial trails cuts through the wilderness, aiding navigation. Footprints are sometimes seen along these paths, though the source is never found. The forest is deeply unnatural. Its trees are composed of a dark, unidentified material resembling wood, lined with razor-thin thorns capable of slicing through solid steel. They produce no leaves, no fruit, and grow in suffocating density, making off-trail movement nearly impossible. In rare cases, especially during earthquakes or strong vibrations, some trees collapse and shoot splinters outward like shrapnel. A '''M.E.G. outpost''' stationed within the level established in a subterranean cave ten meters beneath the surface has determined that any safe traversal would require specialized armor. However, such equipment remains theoretical, as nothing constructed so far has proven viable against the thorns. Resources within Desolate White are virtually nonexistent. The deep, heavy snow blankets all potential vegetation, and the extreme cold renders most conventional technology and survival tools unreliable. Foraging for sustenance is impossible. This level lacks a functioning day-night cycle. The sky is forever in thick, dull overcast. There’s no sun, no moon, just thick, gray clouds that stretch far beyond the range of sight. Time feels motionless. Earthquakes, sudden windstorms, and random temperature shifts persist, making the area volatile and unpredictable. Occasionally, the trails pass by ancient ruins and abandoned campsites. These structures are decayed beyond recognition and appear to have been untouched for centuries. Scattered debris expired food, rusted utensils, toothbrushes—can sometimes be found. Judging by architecture alone, the ruins are estimated to be over 300 years old. No identifying documents or markings have been recovered. Further beyond the tree line, massive open fields some spanning over '''30 kilometers,''' have been documented, beginning around '''100 kilometers''' from the initial entry point. In some rare areas, the snow thins, revealing patches of blackened soil. Beneath these are tufts of what appears to be scorched, tar-like grass. Some believe these burns may be remnants of fires or lightning strikes, while others suggest they relate to the spontaneous appearance of malformed snowmen found throughout the region. The most notable phenomenon in Level 415 is the appearance of strange, floating lights above the clouds. These lights flicker in and out, sometimes hovering for hours before vanishing without a trace. Some believe they are orbiting satellites, others suspect weather anomalies or surveillance constructs.{{Blurimage|file=Level lifeless 5751.png|caption=Improvised image of the lights.}}Despite the uncertainty, the lights are known to cause severe adverse effects. Direct exposure has led to exhaustion, spatial disorientation, temporary blindness, dehydration, and memory gaps. In some cases, the lights generate sudden waves of heat or cold, warping the surrounding temperature. These spikes range from -43°C to 70°C (-45.4°F to 158°F). Most lights remain temperature-neutral, but the volatile ones are dangerous. Avoiding the lights is critical. When dim, they can usually be bypassed without issue. But once they brighten, they become a threat. Hiding behind trees, inside ruins, or even beneath thick brush may reduce their effects. Symptoms vary in length and intensity, lasting from seconds to several hours. Rarely, massive shapes can be seen behind the clouds—brief silhouettes of what look like alien spacecraft or mechanical behemoths. They move fast, vanish quickly, and only partial outlines or flashes of movement are visible. These shapes emit mechanical sounds, sharp bursts of static, deep engine groans, and chaotic audio patterns. Volume ranges from a whisper to over 100 decibels. Exposure causes nausea, headaches, and dizziness. Their nature, origin, and intent remain unknown. '''The Laboratory''' Far beyond the frozen forests, on the edge of a vast frozen ocean, lies an abandoned laboratory. This facility once served as a research site for the level’s anomalous features. The group behind its construction is unknown. Now in near-total ruin, the lab’s interior is barely navigable. Most tech is corroded and inert. Recovered documents mention testing involving entities, experimental weapons, and prototype armor. One of the lab’s last experiments was a project known as '''Project Snowman'''. Reports suggest the subject broke containment, wreaked havoc, and caused the facility’s collapse. Today, the lab remains dead silent. Still, rumors persist that Project Snowman wasn’t the only entity to escape. Other entities may still be roaming the level.

Entities

----'''Project Snowman''' commonly referred to as ''the Snowman'' is a moderately dangerous entity. Its height fluctuates, but most stand around two meters tall. They have a carrot for a nose, chunks of coal or rock for eyes, and sometimes wear top hats. Those with top hats are far more aggressive, up to six times stronger than a human. They wield their noses like blades but move slowly. Hatless Snowmen are less violent but still dangerous, especially to unarmed travelers. Special heat-based prototype weapons found in the ruins of the lab seem to have been built specifically to counter them. However, these weapons have proven largely ineffective in practice. Ordinary weapons are useless as well; the snow forming their bodies is absurdly durable. These Snowmen appear whenever one of the floating lights manifests. Within a one-mile radius, a Snowman will begin to form. No two are ever identical. In a rare documented case, an M.E.G. operative used a customized heat weapon on one. As the snow melted away, it revealed a thick layer of pitch-black skin beneath, similar in appearance to the tar-colored grass. The connection is unconfirmed, but some speculate that these creatures are a fusion of the level’s organic and anomalous matter. Since the prototype heat weapons are unreliable and mostly ineffective, wanderers are advised to bring Molotovs or any object that can ignite from a distance. Once a Snowman melts, it disappears into the ground without a trace.

Entrances & Exits

----

Entrances

* Entering this level is mostly random, but the chance increases once one is in a snowy level. * Going up a snow-covered staircase on [[level 410]] will lead one here. * No clipping while sliding on [[Level 210]] will lead one here. * Walking along the highway long enough in [[Level 395]] can sometimes lead one here.

Exits

* Wandering long enough to the South will cause the snow to fade away, leading the traveler to [[level 416]]. * Venturing deep inside a cave presents a rare opportunity for the traveler to reach [[Level 8|level 8.]] * Breaking a portion of the ice on a frozen river, lake, or ocean and jumping into the freezing water has a 50/50 chance of sending someone to either [[level 202]] or [[Level 79|level 79.]] {{Collapse|num=2|initial='''> Open Author & licensing information'''|final='''< Close Author & licensing information'''|body= Author: [[User:The Lone Wanderer12313|TLW12313]]
